enonce.texte:
Instructions: Look at the pictures and write the name of the job. Choose from the words in the box.

Word Box: teacher, doctor, farmer, nurse, driver, shopkeeper

1. This person helps sick people in a hospital. ________

2. This person grows crops and raises animals. ________

3. This person teaches students in a school. ________

4. This person sells things in a shop. ________

5. This person drives a bus or a taxi. ________

questions.texte: 1. Identify the job from the description: "This person helps sick people in a hospital."
2. Identify the job from the description: "This person grows crops and raises animals."
3. Identify the job from the description: "This person teaches students in a school."
4. Identify the job from the description: "This person sells things in a shop."
5. Identify the job from the description: "This person drives a bus or a taxi."

intitule: Exercise 2: Reading Comprehension – What People Do

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the text below carefully and answer the questions.

People in Our Community

In our community, there are many people with different jobs. Mr. Ndi is a teacher. He teaches English at the local school. He helps students read and write. Mrs. Ngo is a doctor. She works at the health center. She examines patients and gives them medicine. Peter is a farmer. He grows vegetables and raises chickens. He sells his produce at the market. Amina is a shopkeeper. She sells bread, sugar, and other groceries. She is very friendly. All these people are important in our community.

questions.texte: 1. Identify the job of Mr. Ndi.
2. Describe what Mrs. Ngo does at the health center.
questions.reponse: Answer: She examines patients and gives them medicine.

questions.texte: 3. List the things that Peter grows and raises.
questions.reponse: Answer: Peter grows vegetables and raises chickens.

questions.texte: 4. Explain why all these people are important in the community.
questions.reponse: Answer: They are important because they help the community in different ways: they teach, heal, produce food, and sell goods.

enonce.texte: Instructions: Complete the sentences with the correct form of the verb in brackets. Use the simple present tense for he/she/it.

questions.texte: 1. Mr. Ndi ________ (teach) English at the school.
2. A doctor ________ (help) sick people.
3. The farmer ________ (sell) vegetables at the market.
4. She ________ (drive) a bus every day.
5. My mother ________ (work) in a bank.

intitule: Exercise 4: Grammar – Using Can and May

questions.texte: 1. A doctor ________ help you when you are sick.
2. ________ I help you, sir? (polite offer)
3. A teacher ________ explain difficult topics to students.
4. ________ I speak to the doctor, please?

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Choose a job you know well (e.g., your parent's job, a teacher's job, a farmer's job). Write a short paragraph of about 60-80 words describing what the person does. Use the simple present tense and include the following:

  • The name of the job
  • What the person does (tasks)
  • One sentence with can and one with may

questions.texte: 1. Write a short paragraph describing a job.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:
My mother is a nurse. She works at the local hospital. She takes care of sick people. She gives them medicine and checks their temperature. She can help people feel better. May I ask you a question? She is very kind and always smiles. I am proud of her because she saves lives every day.

intitule: Exercise 6: Role-Play – At the Health Center

enonce.texte:
Instructions: Read the dialogue below and complete it with the missing words. Then, practice the dialogue with a partner.

Doctor: Good morning! How ________ you feeling?

Patient: I ________ not feeling well. I have a headache.

Doctor: ________ I examine you? Please sit down.

Patient: Thank you, doctor. ________ you help me?

Doctor: Of course. You ________ take some medicine. You ________ rest for two days.

questions.texte: 1. Complete the dialogue with the correct words.
questions.reponse:
Answer:
Doctor: Good morning! How are you feeling?
Patient: I am not feeling well. I have a headache.
Doctor: May I examine you? Please sit down.
Patient: Thank you, doctor. Can you help me?
Doctor: Of course. You can take some medicine. You may rest for two days.

questions.texte: 2. Role-play the dialogue with a partner. One person is the doctor, the other is the patient.
questions.reponse: Answer: Performance will be assessed based on correct pronunciation and use of expressions.
